texto: Y’all ever seen that 'monkeys typing in a room for eternity would eventually create the works of Shakespeare' quote? Well, one time Drew got high and stated, 'Wait, that happened already. We’re monkeys, and space is eternity, and we typed, and it happened.' He insisted we put it in the book.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Trae Crowder que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Y’all ever seen that 'monkeys typing in a room for eternity would eventually create the works of …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, humorous-quotes, monkeys, com foco em trabalho e reflexao.

texto: FIRST AMENDMENT No one shall give a damn about your religion. Freedom of speech doesn’t mean you can say whatever you want about prayer and God and rules and gays, and everyone else has to take it. It means you can say whatever you want about all that, but I can then say back to you, “Your church is a cult, and all the women in it are fat.” So suck it up, Brother Daryl.
explicacao: Citação de Trae Crowder sobre temas ligados a fe. “FIRST AMENDMENT No one shall give a damn about your religion. Freedom of speech doesn’t mean you …” articula fe, church, freedom-of-thought em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como termino e freedom-of-thought.

texto: Slavery happened. That flag stands for segregation. We have monuments to Civil War generals and slave owners, as well as preserved plantations. But we have only one slavery museum, and that was built by a private citizen. We have no national or federal slavery museum. There is no government-funded slavery museum. A proposal to put one in Virginia came through in 2001 and went unfunded and failed. Another one in Richmond reached a similar fate. This is absolutely shameful.

texto: Men justify the women taking care of the kids and the house and holding down a job because, after all, men are the primary breadwinners in the family. But that’s because we fucking pay women less! Men claiming superiority over women because men make more money than they do is like claiming you’re stronger than a lion that you tranquilized and put in a cage. Sure, you’re in a better spot now—but how ’bout you unchain the beast and see what happens?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Trae Crowder: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Men justify the women taking care of the kids and the house and holding down a job because, after…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, equal-rights, female-roles pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de female-roles e sexism.
